titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/232

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=192 status=live modified=2017-02-28 15:43:57.748983+00 | translation moa=193 status=live modified=2017-02-28 15:44:00.73171+00

div.p:
M Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus totius terre sue . salutem . Sciant Clerici ⁊
Laici me in perpetuam elemosinam dedisse ⁊ hac mea carta confirmasse Deo ⁊ Sancte
Cruci de Edeneb’ ⁊ Canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus unum toftum plenarium in
in Burgo meo de Inuerkethin . Tenendum sibi ita libere ⁊ quiete  sicut aliquod toftum
in eodem Burgo liberius ⁊ quietius in elemosinam tenetur ⁊ possidetur . Testibus Eng’ Cancellario
Waltero filio Alani dapifero . Rob’ Auenel . Apud Linlidcu .

Malcolm king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land Greeting: Let the clergy and laymen know that I have given in perpetual alms and by this my charter made firm to God and to Holyrood at Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there one full toft in my burgh of Inverkeithing. For them to hold as freely and quietly as any toft in the same burgh is freely and quietly held and possessed in alms. As witnesses, Ingram the chancellor, Walter the steward, son of Alan, Robert Avenel. At Linlithgow.
